rulings.Case Title: LONNA WAIS VS. ROBERT L FARMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Re Deft Robert Farmer'S Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 1: Defendants ROBERT FARMER'S, ROBERT WEAVER'S, and FARMER & RIDLEY LLP'S Motion For Protective Order Re Defendant Robert Farmer's Deposition Pro Tem Judge Michele Wolf, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for a protective order is granted. Mr. Dillick may depose Robert Farmer for two 3-1/2 sessions over two consecutive days to be completed by February 1, 2014 in Los Angeles at Robert Farmer's office. Plaintiff may move this court for more cross-examination time following Mr. Farmer's deposition if necessary. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to michelerwolf@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNIE L. BROWN VS. DENNIS J. HERRERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant produces prima facie evidence that the alleged dangerous condition was not caused by a negligent, or wrongful act or omission by its employees within the scope of employment, or that Defendant had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition. (Gov. Code §835; See also Declaration of Gerchow in Support of Motion, Exh. D, Nos. 25-27; Declaration of Popp in Support of Motion, 2, 5-6; Declaration of Hao in Support of Motion, 3-4, Exhs. B, C.) Consequently the burden shifts to Plaintiff. Plaintiff has not demonstrated a triable issue of material facts exists. Plaintiff relies on inadmissible evidence to dispute the material facts. (See Declaration of Brown in Support of Opposition, 11; Declaration of Pipkin in Support of Opposition, 8.) "Evidence that gives rise to no more than mere speculation is insufficient to establish a triable issue of material fact." (Sangster v. Paetkau (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 151, 163.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JIANJUN XIE VS. VICENTE NAVARRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT VICENTE NAVARRO's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant's motion to set aside the default is granted. Defendant's motion to dismiss is granted. CCP § 473(d) provides that a void judgment may be set aside at any time. A judgment may be void due to improper service of summons or entry of default without proper service of a statement of damages. (Sakaguchi v. Sakaguchi (2009) 173 Cal.App.4th 852, 858.) Service was not properly effectuated and Defendant did not receive actual notice of the action. In order to properly effectuate substitute service, the complaint and summons must be left with a competent member of the household. (CCP § 415.20.) Plaintiff's declaration of service states that the papers were nailed to the door, not left with a member of the household. Additionally, Plaintiff did not properly serve Defendant with a statement of damages pursuant to CCP § 425.11(d)(1), which requires that a statement of damages be served on a defendant who has not yet appeared in the same manner as a summons. Mailing the statement is insufficient service. The court grants Defendant's motion to dismiss pursuant to CCP § 583.420, as Defendant has not been served within two years of the commencement of the action. Defendant has met his burden in showing that Plaintiff was not diligent in prosecuting the action. Defendant will be unduly prejudiced in his defense of this matter over four years after the subject incident. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Ofmotion And Motion To Seal X-Complaint Adn Any Future Filings Disclosing Attorney-Client Communications
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 7, PLAINTIFF BENTLY RESERVE, L.P., A CALIFORIA LIMITED, BENTLY NOB HILL, L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED, CHRISTOPHER BENTLY, AN INDIVIDUAL, AMBER-MARIE BENTLY, AN INDIVIDUAL Motion To Seal X-Complaint Adn Any Future Filings Disclosing Attorney-Client Communications. rulings.Case Title: JOHN C. MITCHELL VS. BARRELIER ASSOCIATES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT BARRELIER ASSOCIATES, DON JOSE BARRELIER DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer is overruled as to the second cause of action, and sustained with leave to amend as to the first, third, and fourth causes of action. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff has alleged facts that Defendants owed a duty of care to Plaintiff as a third party to the real property transaction, and that Defendants breached this duty when they failed to advise their clients to read the inspection report and not to misrepresent or fail to disclose facts material facts regarding the transaction. (See First Amended Complaint, 63; see also Norman I. Krug Real Estate Investments, Inc. v. Praszker (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 35, 42.) Defendants' demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the first cause of action. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts giving rise to Defendants' legal duty to indemnify Plaintiff. Defendant's demurrers to third and fourth causes of action are sustained. Both causes of action allege facts regarding Defendants' intentional misrepresentation. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave, if he can do so in good faith, to allege facts giving rise to Defendants' negligent and intentional misrepresentation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI, DBA PROFESSIONAL CONCRETE WORK VS. NILS WELIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT NILS WELIN Re-Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Default Judgment. Granted, in part, as to Interrogatory no. 6. 11, 12, 13, 18, 14, 21, and denied as to Interrogatory no. 7. For Interrogatory no. 6, objections are sustained in part and overruled for the time period of July 30, 2010 to September 15, 2011. For Interrogatory no.7, objects are sustained. For Interrogatory no. 11, plaintiff's objections are sustained as to the spouse/domestic partners privacy and objections to providing plaintiff's income is overruled. For Interrogatory no. 12. Plaintiff's objections are overruled in their entirety. For Interrogatory no. 13, plaintiff's objection are sustained in part and overruled in part. Plaintiff is only required to provide the account numbers subject to a protective order. For Interrogatory no. 18 plaintiff's objections are sustained for years 2009 and overruled for years 2010 and continuing. For RFP no 14, plaintiff's objections are sustained for year 2009 and overruled for years 2010 and continuing. For RFP 21, plaintiff's objection are sustained for years 2007, 2008 and 2009 and overruled for years 2010 and continuing. Within 20 days, Plaintiff shall provide further responses and produce responsive documents in accordance with the Court's ruling on the objections. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Its Request For Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Its Request For Production Of Documents And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part. Plaintiff shall provide further responses, without objection, to the Requests from the Request for Production, Set One, as identified and limited below, within 15 days of notice of entry of this order, and shall produce any further documents in plaintiff's (and her attorney's) possession or control, but not already produced, at the office of counsel for Bank of the West (650 California St., 20th Flr., San Francisco CA) at 10:00 a.m., 5 court days thereafter. For Request nos. 1, 5, 6: Limited to the period 10/01/2011 to 8/31/12. For Request nos. 5, 6, the terms "refer to, or RELATE TO" are stricken. For Request nos. 7, 16: Without limitation. For Request nos. 25-28: Limited to the period August 1, 2012 to present. Further, for Request no. 26, limited to job applications, personnel policies, employee handbooks, benefit plans, job evaluations, pay stubs and job descriptions. Further, for Request no. 27, limited to benefit plans, pay stubs, job descriptions, contracts and description of work performed. Further, for Request no. 28, limited to claims for health care or disability benefits. For Request nos. 26 - 28, the terms "refer to, or RELATE TO" are stricken. For Request nos. 29, 30, 31: Limited to the period October 1, 2010 to present. Further, for Request no. 30, 32, the terms "refer to, or RELATE TO" are stricken. Further, for Request no. 31, limited to any communications between plaintiff and any physician, psychiatrist, psychologist, nurse, counselor, therapist, or other practitioner of the healing arts related to an symptom, syndrom, illness, disease or injury suffered as a result of the alleged conduct of defendants. (Tylo v. Sup.Ct. (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 1379, 1388.) Request 29 is also limited to those medical conditions Plaintiff has put at issue, specifically gastro-intestinal complaints, depression, and emotional distress. Denied as to: Request nos. 18, 21, 32, 34: Requests are not sufficiently particularized. (Calcor Space Facility, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 216, 222.) Further, for Request no. 34, the request is too broad. (Tylo, supra, 55 Cal.App.4th at 1388._ Req. No. 24: Tax payer privilege has not been waived. Motion for Sanctions is denied. No sanctions are awarded to either party. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Special Interrogatories And Award Of Monetary Sanction Against Deft And Its Counsel Littler Mendelson In The Amouht Of $2,665
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL Notice Of Motion To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Special Interrogatories And Award Of Monetary Sanction Against Deft And Its Counsel Littler Mendelson In The Amouht Of $2,665. Defendant's general objections and specific objections violated CCP §2030.300(a)(3) because they are too general and are sanctionable "boilerplate" per Korea Data Systems Co. Ltd. v. Sup. Ct. (Amazing Technologies Corp.) (1997) 51 CA4th, 1513, 1516. Defendant shall answer Special Interrogatories 1-6, inclusive, without objection within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. With respect to contact information requested in Form Interrogatories (Employment) 207.2 and 217.1, the motion is Denied. The witnesses are current or former officers, managers, or employees who are percipient witnesses and whose "act[s] or failure to act in connection with the matter . . . may bind the corporation, be imputed to it, or constitute an admission of the corporation for purposes of establishing liability." (Triple A Machine Shop, Inc. v. State of California (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 131, 140.) Accordingly, they may not be contacted by plaintiff's counsel independently by virtue of RPC 2-100(B)(2). plaintiff can take their depositions upon notice to defendant's counsel. Hence, their privacy rights outweigh plaintiff's interest in their contact information. Life Technologies Corp. v. Superior Court (Timothy H. Joyce) (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 640, 655. If plaintiff does not produce former employees or officers for deposition upon notice, then plaintiff is entitled to their contact information, subject to an appropriately drafted confidentiality protective order or agreement. Motion for sanctions is Denied. No sanctions are awarded to either party. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL MENDOZA, AN INDIVIDUAL VS. JOSEPH TUMANG, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Depo Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 5: Plaintiff MANUEL MENDOZA'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoenas Pro Tem Judge Michele Wolf, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL LEE MCKINNEY VS. LUCY C. PASTRANA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: DUE TO A COMPUTER PROBLEM, THE TENTATIVE RULINGS WERE NOT ISSUED TIMELY. Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 7, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT LUCY PASTRANA DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer to the second cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given 10 days leave, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts that are not duplicative of Plaintiff's first cause of action to quiet title. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for ouster. (Complaint, 12, 15, 16, 24; see also Zaslow v. Kroenert (1946) 29 Cal.2d 541, 548; Kapner v. Meadowlark Ranch Ass'n (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 1182, 1189.) Defendant's demurrer for uncertainty is overruled as it is not dispositive of Plaintiff's cause of action for partition. (See Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc., 14 CA4th 612, 617 (cited in Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 7(I)-A.)) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
